title: EU BPA ban reaches its main food-contact packaging transition deadline
summary: Food Packaging Forum reports that the main EU transition period for bisphenol A in food-contact materials ended on July 20, 2026. Most single-use and reusable food-contact articles manufactured with BPA can no longer be newly placed on the EU market, while defined sell-through and longer technical derogation timelines still apply for selected articles.
buyerTakeaway: For EU-bound baking cups, greaseproof paper packs and printed food paper items, ask suppliers to refresh BPA and related-bisphenol declarations against Regulation (EU) 2024/3190 and separate newly placed stock from articles already on the market before the deadline.

title: Indonesia updates food-contact packaging rules with paper and board in scope
summary: Food Packaging Forum reports that Indonesia's BPOM adopted Regulation No. 11 of 2026 on food packaging, replacing the 2019 rule. The regulation covers paper and board alongside plastics, metals, glass and multilayer materials, with migration requirements, positive and prohibited substance lists, and provisions for reusable and recycled packaging.
buyerTakeaway: For Southeast Asia-bound baking paper, greaseproof wraps and printed food paper packs, confirm migration evidence, substance-list checks and updated supplier declarations before the 12-month transition window closes.

title: European Commission updates EUDR scope and digital tools before year-end application
summary: The European Commission adopted measures to support EUDR implementation, including an updated product-scope Delegated Act and technical rules for the Information System used for due diligence statements and simplified declarations. The Commission says the changes are intended to give businesses more legal certainty before application begins at the end of 2026.
buyerTakeaway: For EU paper packaging orders, re-check whether the SKU is sold as a covered product or only used as carrier packaging, and prepare supplier-origin, fiber and due-diligence records in a format that can support the EUDR Information System.

title: FDA decision on PFAS in food raises the bar for packaging evidence
summary: The Guardian reports that the U.S. FDA rejected a petition seeking enforceable PFAS limits in food while indicating it may work on action levels. The article also notes that PFAS can enter the food system through several routes, including packaging, making documented source control important for food brands.
buyerTakeaway: U.S. buyers should keep finished-item PFAS documentation for greaseproof paper, baking paper and foodservice packs even where federal food thresholds remain unsettled. Supplier letters should be backed by formulation controls or testing, not only marketing claims.
imageAlt: Article image from The Guardian's report on the FDA decision about PFAS limits in food

title: Dessert packaging buyers prioritize freshness, resealability and recyclability
summary: Packaging Insights reports that dessert and sweet snack packs are being redesigned around product protection and circularity. Suppliers point to resealable formats, barrier performance, easy opening and recyclable monomaterial structures as brands work to protect texture while meeting consumer and regulatory expectations.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery cookies, muffins and sweet snack paper formats, define the product-protection target before changing the pack. Ask suppliers to document grease and moisture control, seal performance, recyclability claims, print system and line compatibility together.
imageAlt: Cookie packaging image published with Packaging Insights' report on dessert packaging trends

title: BPA, heavy-metal and PPWR PFAS deadlines converge for food-contact packs
summary: Pack-Lab highlights two near-term EU compliance steps: the bisphenol restriction under Regulation (EU) 2024/3190 from 20 July 2026 for single-use food-contact materials and PPWR Article 5 chemical requirements from 12 August 2026. The article emphasizes analytical evidence for bisphenols, heavy metals and PFAS instead of relying on documentation alone.
buyerTakeaway: For EU-bound baking cups, greaseproof paper and printed food paper packaging, align supplier declarations with material tests for BPA-related bisphenols, total heavy metals, individual PFAS, summed PFAS and total fluorine before shipment.

title: Food-contact ink guidance reinforces migration control for printed paper packs
summary: Packaging Technology Today explains that food-contact packaging materials must be selected with migration, odor, substrate behavior and regulatory documentation in mind. The article highlights migration-optimized inks as one way to reduce set-off and contamination risk when printed packs sit close to food.
buyerTakeaway: For custom cupcake liners, greaseproof wraps and bakery cartons, ask suppliers to identify the ink system, intended food-contact condition, migration assumptions and odor-control checks before approving artwork for production.
imageAlt: Printed food packaging image published with Packaging Technology Today's article on food-contact material selection

title: Taller cupcake liners become a simple bakery-style performance upgrade
summary: Good Housekeeping reports that baking expert Dan Langan recommends taller cupcake liners to help batter rise upward, reduce spreading and create a more polished bakery-style dome. The article notes that 1.5 inch liners can also help contain recipes that spill over in shorter paper cups.
buyerTakeaway: For retail and bakery cupcake liner assortments, consider offering taller paper baking cups alongside standard sizes, with clear height, diameter, capacity and pan-fit specifications so buyers can match presentation goals to recipes and equipment.

title: Air fryer paper liner demand rewards standardized sizes and food-contact claims
summary: Future Market Insights says the air fryer paper liners market is forecast to grow through 2036, with 7.0 to 9.0 inch liners expected to hold half of the size segment in 2026. The report points to recurring consumable demand, online bulk buying, perforation patterns and rising interest in unbleached or chemical-free paper liner products.
buyerTakeaway: For private-label air fryer liners, prioritize mainstream 7 to 9 inch SKUs, perforation consistency, heat-use instructions, food-contact declarations and claims such as unbleached or PFAS-free that can be supported by real test records.

title: BfR paper-and-board revision puts substance lists under closer review
summary: PackagingLaw.com reports that Germany's BfR opened a consultation on Recommendation XXXVI for paper and board food-contact materials, integrating several paper recommendations into one draft and adding clearer tabular substance entries with CAS numbers where available. BfR recommendations are not binding law, but they are widely used for paper, board and other materials without EU-harmonized specific measures, while LFGB Section 31 restricts unsafe articles on the German market.
buyerTakeaway: For greaseproof paper, baking cups, liners and printed paper packaging sold into Germany or benchmarked against BfR, ask suppliers to map additives, wet-strength agents, coatings, colorants and CAS numbers against the latest Recommendation XXXVI draft and retain LFGB-ready declarations.
